steps.title: Apply the Zero Product Property
steps.content:
The equation $(x+2)(x-5)(x+9)=0$ is already factored. Since the product equals zero, at least one factor must equal zero. Setting each factor equal to zero:
$$x + 2 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -2$$
$$x - 5 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 5$$
$$x + 9 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -9$$
The three solutions are $-2, 5,$ and $-9$.

steps.title: Identify the Positive Solution
steps.content: The question asks for a positive solution. Among $\{-2, 5, -9\}$, only \$5$ is positive. That matches choice C — C is correct.
